summary: Revamp excalidraw-diagram-skill into a React-code-based animated visualizer skill with Motion + Remotion, multi-agent validation, CAH-style setup, step-by-step commits
prompt_verbatim:
And what we will do is we will create a skill in there, a Claude skill or codex skill, which we created, in which we give this to our agent to draw and visualize. It is purely based on converse and combine and now I will completely rewrite it. We will revamp it, a new version, and in this version it will be purely a react-based element-based skill, with no canvas or our agent designing the code itself, the SVG diagram flow in pure HTML, CSS, and tell wind scripts or whatever it needs. We will give it library packages, animation packages, frame or motion, and all so it will do everything on its own.

We will have animations, video. We will give it remotion axes as well to generate videos in the browser itself as a video player guide and all to create this, like a proper flow. Visualize spawn multiple team agents and validate the idea approaches like a reviewer, a QA architecture, and all, verify this, run this, go through each step commit exactly, or just create a purely new skill. The new skill will be like a visualizer or explainer or expander, something. Choose a very unique name.

Create a folder, initialize, set up the whole project or whatever, like the way we do pre-commit hooks scripts. Each file should have about me flows for each step, which we do for reference. You can check the documents folder. We have a card against humanity in the project folder documents project card amount folder. We had that to use that as a reference for the steps with setup and all, so create the similar to this itself. We will be very efficient, very purely, and this should be able to be installed as a skill so we can just run a scale or whatever tool. It should be so it is given to the cloud or any agents a contest so the agent can use this and visualize the user flow and all.

rationale: User asked for a very unique name in the visualizer/explainer space. 'explanimate' is a portmanteau that states the skill's exact job — explain by animating — is brandable, has near-zero collision, and works as both a folder name and a SKILL.md frontmatter name.

summary: v2 inversion: scenes are React code, not serialized canvas JSON
decision: The agent writes React components (SVG + Tailwind + Motion) instead of emitting Excalidraw element JSON
options: Keep JSON elements with a richer schema
React code authored by the agent
rationale: v1's pain: coordinate math in JSON, no reusable abstractions, no animation, output-token ceiling on large diagrams. Code gives composition (primitives), real layout engines (flex/grid/viewBox), first-class animation, and diffs instead of full regeneration. A DSL was rejected because it caps expressiveness — the user explicitly wants the agent to design freely.

summary: Two animation drivers over one set of animation-agnostic primitives
decision: Motion (motion/react) drives interactive scenes; Remotion's frame clock (useCurrentFrame/interpolate/spring) drives video compositions; shared primitives accept progress props and stay driver-agnostic
rationale: Remotion renders frame-by-frame in a headless browser; Motion's time-based springs/transitions do not advance with Remotion's frame clock, so videos would render with frozen or torn animation. Driving everything by progress props would make interactive scenes needlessly verbose. Splitting drivers keeps each environment idiomatic while primitives stay shared.

summary: Distribution: bundled template + init script, one studio per consumer project
decision: Skill ships templates/studio (Vite+React19+TS+Tailwind v4+Motion+Remotion); scripts/init.mjs copies it into the consumer project as ./explanimate-studio
options: Scaffold per project from bundled template
Single global studio shared across projects
rationale: A per-project studio accumulates that project's scenes as a living gallery, installs deps once, and the dev server doubles as the deliverable. A remote scaffolder adds network+publish overhead for no gain in an agent-skill context; a global studio mixes unrelated projects' scenes and breaks per-project version pinning.

decision: git init; husky pre-commit (lint-staged: ABOUTME headers + prettier), commit-msg (commitlint conventional, scoped), pre-push (pnpm validate); check-headers.mjs ported from card-against-humanity
rationale: User explicitly asked to mirror the card-against-humanity setup: pre-commit hook scripts, ABOUTME headers on every file, step-by-step conventional commits.

summary: Architect agent spawned to validate the design before build
decision: Background architect agent reviews docs/architecture/overview.md against v1 SKILL.md and CAH conventions; verdict gates the build
options: Review design first, QA after build
rationale: User asked for multi-agent validation (reviewer, QA, architecture). Architecture review is cheapest before the template exists; QA and code review run after the artifacts exist.

summary: Architect review agent died on a session limit before producing a verdict
rationale: Subagent hit the account's session cap mid-review. Architecture diligence done inline instead (determinism split, version lockstep, screenshot settling all pre-mitigated in the design); reviewer/QA agents retried post-build.

decision: Progressive disclosure: SKILL.md stays ~200 lines (workflow + API contract + checklist); depth lives in references/ loaded on demand
rationale: v1 was a 553-line monolith the agent had to hold entirely. Splitting keeps the always-loaded surface small while patterns/motion/video guidance loads only when relevant.

summary: Stage takes an explicit scale prop for video; auto-fit stays browser-only
decision: Stage{scale} bypasses the ResizeObserver; compositions pass scale={1.5} to map 1280×720 scene coords onto 1920×1080
options: Duplicate layouts at video resolution with font overrides
rationale: ResizeObserver fires async — not frame-deterministic under Remotion's screenshot clock, risking frames captured at scale=1. Explicit scale also lets compositions reuse scene coordinates and typography verbatim; the first composition draft proved the alternative (per-element font overrides) is unmaintainable.

summary: Dogfood: the verify loop caught two real defects in its own reference scene
decision: Fixed: verify card clipped at the right stage edge (x 1064→1040); Label shrink-to-fit wrapping near stage edges (width:max-content on the absolute wrapper)
rationale: First shoot of the reference scene surfaced both — exactly the class of defect the loop exists to catch. The Label fix went into the primitive, not the scene, so every future scene inherits it.

summary: Template verified end-to-end; consumer journey tested
decision: templates/studio: pnpm install (remotion lockstep at 4.0.474), typecheck clean, vite build clean, shoot→PNG audited, Remotion stills f45/f140/f290 show correct mid-draw determinism, full 300-frame 1080p MP4 rendered (1MB). init.mjs scaffold tested in /tmp: install+typecheck+shoot green; clobber guard refuses non-empty target without --force.
skill-reviewer (consumer simulation: cross-check every doc claim against template source) + qa-reviewer (bug hunt in primitives/scripts with a live scaffolded studio to reproduce against)
rationale: User asked for multi-agent validation (reviewer, QA, architecture). Post-build panel verifies artifacts, not intentions; findings get fixed and committed before release.

summary: Both panel agents killed mid-run (session limits); audits completed inline against their hunt lists
rationale: The reviewer/QA agent prompts enumerated concrete suspects; each was verified or refuted empirically in the main loop instead: multi-scene shoot staleness (CONFIRMED, fixed), ortho vertical routing (CONFIRMED, fixed), video:still default output (verified works), init.mjs filter semantics (verified), draw contracts in shot mode (verified via screenshots).

summary: Review findings fixed and verified: shoot staleness, ortho V-H-V, four doc/API mismatches
decision: shoot.mjs blanks the page between scenes (hash-only navigation never reloads, so the previous scene's ready-marker could satisfy the wait); Edge ortho elbows along the dominant axis; SKILL.md table corrected (Stage scale not title, Node danger kind, Label/Dot delay+appear); design-language.md no longer references the nonexistent bg-node-process utility
rationale: Proven with a two-scene consumer studio in /tmp: ortho-test.png shows correct vertical arrows and a non-stale second capture. Reference scene reshoots byte-identical to the committed example — no regression, and the capture pipeline itself is deterministic.
